016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.jsonrpc;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.base.Network;
019import org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.pojo.ChainTip;
020import org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.BitcoinClient;
021import io.reactivex.rxjava3.core.Flowable;
022import org.consensusj.bitcoin.jsonrpc.BitcoinExtendedClient;
023import org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.ChainTipPublisher;
024import org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.ChainTipService;
025import org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.zeromq.RxBitcoinZmqService;
026import org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcTransport;
027import org.consensusj.rx.jsonrpc.RxJsonRpcClient;
028import org.reactivestreams.Publisher;
029
030import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
031import java.net.URI;
032import java.time.Duration;
033import java.util.concurrent.CompletionStage;
034import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
035import java.util.function.Supplier;
036
037/**
038 * A {@link BitcoinClient} enhanced with Reactive features. Can use either ZeroMQ or polling
039 * to implement {@link ChainTipService}.
040 * <p>
041 * TODO: answer the below questions
042 * <p>
043 * Should this class eventually implement {@link org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.RxBlockchainService}
044 * or {@link org.consensusj.bitcoin.rx.RxBlockchainBinaryService}?
045 * <p>
046 * Should this class be renamed to {@code RxBitcoinJsonRpcClient} and the {@code RxBitcoinClient} interface be moved
047 * to {@code cj-btc-rx?}
048 */
049public class RxBitcoinClient extends BitcoinExtendedClient implements ChainTipService, RxJsonRpcClient {
050    private final boolean useZmq;
051    private /* Lazy */ ChainTipService chainTipService;
052
053    public RxBitcoinClient(Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword) {
054        this(network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword, true);
055    }
056
057    public RxBitcoinClient(Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword, boolean useZmq) {
058        this(JsonRpcTransport.getDefaultSSLContext(), network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword, useZmq);
059    }
060
061    public RxBitcoinClient(SSLContext sslContext, Network network, URI server, String rpcuser, String rpcpassword, boolean useZmq) {
062        super(sslContext, network, server, rpcuser, rpcpassword);
063        // TODO: Determine if ZMQ is available by querying the server
064        this.useZmq = useZmq;
065        // TODO: Determine whether server is up or down -- add a session re-establishment service
066    }
067
068    private void initChainTipService(Duration timeout) {
069        if (chainTipService == null) {
070            if (useZmq) {
071                log.warn("(useZmq enabled) Initiating server connection (with timeout of {} seconds)", timeout.toSeconds());
072                this.connectToServer(timeout).join();
073                chainTipService = new RxBitcoinZmqService(this);
074            } else {
075                chainTipService = new PollingChainTipServiceImpl(this);
076            }
077        }
078    }
079
080    /**
081     * Repeatedly once-per-new-block poll an async method
082     *
083     * @param supplier A supplier (should be an RPC Method) of a CompletionStage
084     * @param <RSLT> The type of the expected result
085     * @return An Observable for the expected result type, so we can expect one call to {@code onNext} per block.
086     */
087    public <RSLT> Publisher<RSLT> pollOnNewBlockAsync(Supplier<CompletionStage<RSLT>> supplier) {
088        return Flowable.fromPublisher(chainTipPublisher()).flatMap(tip -> pollOnceAsPublisher(supplier));
089    }
090
091    /**
092     * The BitcoinClient must have "connected once" before this is called. This means something else
093     * needs to have called something to do that.
094     * @return a publisher of Chain Tips
095     */
096    @Override
097    public ChainTipPublisher chainTipPublisher() {
098        initChainTipService(Duration.ofMinutes(60));
099        return chainTipService.chainTipPublisher();
100    }
101}
